steps: Prepare the restriction reaction mix for all the samples using the amounts of reagents below: undetermined, nuclease-free water undetermined, CutSmart buffer (10x) undetermined, MseI (10,000 U/ml) undetermined, SbfI (HF) (20,000 U/ml) MseI - 2,500 units New England Biolabs Catalog #R0525L SbfI - 2,500 units New England Biolabs Catalog #R0642L
Combine 9 uL of the restriction reaction product with 1 uL of the P1 adapter. Remember to use a unique P1 adapter for each sample!
Purify the reaction using standard AMPure XP protocol using 0.8x ratio of AMPure and elute in 20 uL of nuclease-free water or 10mM Tris

Prepare the PCR reaction mix for all the samples using the amounts of reagents below: undetermined, nuclease-free water undetermined, Q5 buffer (5x) undetermined, dNTPs (25 mM) undetermined, Forward primer (5 uM) undetermined, Reverse primer (5 uM) undetermined, Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity polymerase Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase - 500 units New England Biolabs Catalog #M0493L
Incubate 3 h at 37°C, heat-kill enzymes for 20 min at 65°C Undetermined, restriction reaction 03:00:00, restriction reaction Undetermined, enzyme inactivation 00:20:00, enzyme inactivation
Combine 6 uL of DNA with 3 uL of the restriction mix.

Prepare the ligation reaction mix for all the samples using the amounts of reagents below: undetermined, CutSmart buffer (10x) undetermined, ATP (100 mM) undetermined, P2 adapter undetermined, T4 DNA ligase (400,000 U/ml) T4 DNA Ligase New England Biolabs Catalog #M0202 ATP Solution (100 mM) Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #R0441 undetermined, nuclease-free water
Add 1.6 uL of the ligation mix to each sample.
Incubate 3 h at 16 °C Undetermined, ligation reaction 03:00:00
Combine 6 uL of purified ligation product with 14 uL of the PCR mix.
PCR program: 30 s at 98°C 20cycles: 20s at 98°C, 30s at 60°C, 40s at 72°C 10 min at 72°C hold at 4°C
Verify the reactions using agarose gel electrophoresis.
Pool the samples, including the 'blank' samples.
Purify the pool using standard AMPure XP protocol using 0.8x ratio of AMPure and elute in 20 uL of nuclease-free water or 10mM Tris.
Perform size selection using PippinPrep instrument or by cutting the desired fragment from the gel.
Check size selection and library fragment size using gel electrophoresis or TapeStation/Bioanalyzer/Fragment Analyzer.
Quantify the amount of DNA using Qubit.
Proceed to HiSeq/MiSeq sequencing. DNA quantity and mean fragment length can be used to calculate the molarity of the library.
creator: Tomasz Suchan
